element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns directional attacks and distance-dependent move variations
Player learns block reduces damage and enables easier counterattacks
Player experiments with character-specific special move inputs
Player learns uppercut knockup enables extended airborne combos
Meter-fill mechanic for bonus points; wood is easiest threshold
Player faces duplicate of chosen character; tests mastery of moveset

element_introduction_order.element: Endurance matches (2v1, health does not regenerate)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must manage cumulative damage across consecutive opponents

element_introduction_order.element: Test Your Might harder materials (stone, steel, ruby, diamond)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive difficulty scaling; diamond only in two-player
Monstrous opponent; undefeated for 500 years in lore
Brutal execution move performed after opponent defeat; character-specific inputs
Final antagonist; controls tournament and threatens Earthrealm

typical_relief_triggers: Test Your Might bonus points earned
typical_tension_triggers: Timer approaching expiration with low health differential
Endurance match begins with cumulative damage
Goro or Shang Tsung match starts
Special move startup lag leaves player vulnerable
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match-based structure with discrete tension peaks (per-round); tension builds within round as health depletes, relieved upon round/match completion. Endurance matches sustain tension across multiple opponents without regeneration. Test Your Might interludes provide brief mechanical respite.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Two-player mode unlocks harder Test Your Might materials (ruby, diamond) not accessible in single-player

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of core mechanics (punch, kick, block, special moves) against basic AI opponents in matches 1-3
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Special move mastery and juggling optimization across matches 4-6; introduction of endurance format and cumulative damage management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Goro sub-boss encounter requires advanced block-counterattack and juggle execution; Shang Tsung final boss fight combines all prior mechanics against tournament antagonist
notable_spikes: Mirror Match after 7 standard matches (player versus self-clone)
First endurance match (3v1 without health regeneration)
Goro encounter (500-year undefeated lore, monstrous frame data)
Shang Tsung final battle (final antagonist, tournament control)

notable_level_patterns.description: Single-player campaign follows rigid sequence: 7 character matches → mirror match → 3 endurance matches → Goro → Shang Tsung. No branching; predetermined opponent order.
Three consecutive 2v1 battles with shared health bar (no regeneration between opponents). Forces resource (health) management and cumulative damage awareness.
Minigame interludes with material difficulty scaling (wood → stone → steel → ruby → diamond). Meter-fill mechanic with increasing point rewards; later materials require longer button-mashing.
Goro positioned as mandatory skill-check before final match against Shang Tsung. Two-stage endgame climax with distinct opponent types.
